claim: File-level SHA-256 content hash exists via computeFileContentHash() and is stored in the content_hash column; canonical-fingerprint.ts should reuse this algorithm rather than re-introducing hashing.

Parent-child chunk row shape (parent_id, chunk_index, chunk_label) and anchor_id column already exist in memory_index from migration v16; new chunk_fingerprint/chunk_kind/chunk_start_line/chunk_end_line are additive extensions, not a new table.
memoization_records table is entirely absent from the schema and all migration paths; no equivalent structure exists in the codebase.
dependency_edges table is entirely absent; no DAG or dependency tracking structure exists in mcp_server.
lib/storage/canonical-fingerprint.ts does not exist; the lib/storage/ directory contains no such file.
lib/storage/memo.ts does not exist; no memo CRUD module is present in lib/storage/.
chunk_fingerprint, chunk_kind, chunk_start_line, chunk_end_line columns absent from memory_index schema and all migrations; confirmed by exhaustive grep with no results.

memory-parser.ts validates and extracts ANCHOR tags (anchor_id) but does not emit per-chunk fingerprints or line spans; fingerprint emission is an extension to existing anchor extraction logic (~80 LOC), not a rewrite.

incremental-index.ts categorizeFilesForIndexing() performs no DAG traversal or dependent invalidation; the function only applies per-file mtime/hash decisions with no knowledge of derived state dependencies.
handlers/memory-index.ts scan planner calls categorizeFilesForIndexing() directly with no memo lookup; there is no pre-scan memo state consultation.
